name: Mushroom Chips (Burke)
recipeInstructions:
1. Hold the mushrooms by their stems and slice each mushroom cap crosswise or horizontally into thin, almost translucent slices.
2. Reserve the stems for another dish.
3. Heat the clarified butter or vegetable oil in a small saucepan over medium heat (do not allow the butter or oil to smoke; a perfect mushroom chip is crisp and tender, but not burned).
4. Add the mushroom slices to the hot butter or oil and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are golden brown, about 6 minutes.
5. Using a slotted spoon, remove the mushroom slices from the pan and drain on paper towels.
6. Season the chips with salt to taste and allow them to rest for at least 1 to 2 hours before serving. They will become crisp during this time.
[per 1/4-cup serving: Calories, 404; protein, 3 grams; carbohydrates, 6 grams; fat, 42 grams; cholesterol, 109 milligrams; sodium, 17 milligrams.]
